subject: Astronomy & Space
title: The Kuiper Belt
description: Learn The Kuiper Belt in one minute.

hook.text: Beyond Neptune lies a vast ring of icy objects stretching billions of km. This is the Kuiper Belt.

buildup.text: Pluto, Eris, and Makemake orbit here among millions of frozen bodies left from the solar system's birth.

discovery.text: Gerard Kuiper predicted the belt in 1951. The first object beyond Pluto was confirmed in 1992.

twist.text: Short-period comets originate here. Jupiter's gravity flings them toward the inner solar system.

climax.text: New Horizons flew past Arrokoth in 2019—a pristine snowman-shaped rock untouched for 4.6 billion years.

punchline.text: The solar system's deep freezer. Pristine leftovers from day one.

quiz.question: What is the Kuiper Belt?
options: A ring of icy objects beyond Neptune left over from the solar system's formation
A gap between Mars and Jupiter filled with rocky asteroids
A cloud of gas surrounding the entire solar system
